title: 10 best herbal essential oils for congestive heart failure
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for congestive heart failure are natural extracts derived from plants that help alleviate symptoms and improve heart function. They work by reducing fluid buildup, improving circulation, and lowering blood pressure. The best essential oils for congestive heart failure include Digitalis purpurea, Crataegus monogyna, Lavandula angustifolia, Ginkgo biloba, Rosmarinus officinalis, Valeriana officinalis, Ruscus aculeatus, Centella asiatica, Astragalus membranaceus, and Panax ginseng. Without proper treatment, congestive heart failure can lead to breathlessness, fatigue, and swelling, severely impacting daily life, restricting activities, and diminishing overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foxglove essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because it has been found to have a significant impact on improving cardiovascular function. The oil contains compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to the development of congestive heart failure. Additionally, the oil's ability to improve blood circulation and lower blood pressure can help alleviate symptoms such as shortness of breath and swelling in the legs.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycoside activity: The main active compounds in Digitalis leaf extract, such as digoxin and digitoxin, help regulate the heartbeat by increasing the force of contractions and decreasing the heart rate, which can be beneficial for patients with congestive heart failure.

Antioxidant properties: Digitalis leaf extract contains antioxidants that may help protect the heart from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation, which are common in patients with congestive heart failure.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The extract has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which can help reduce inflammation and scarring in the heart muscle, a common complication of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of cardiac glycosides, which have been shown to improve heart function and reduce symptoms of congestive heart failure.
Bark: The bark is rich in saponins, which are believed to help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, making it an effective component for medicinal essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh digitalis purpurea leaves and flowers at dawn or late afternoon in a clean environment.
Dry the harvested plant material using a low-temperature drying method within 24 hours of collection.
Grind 20 grams of dried digitalis purpurea into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 100 ml of high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, allowing it to steep for 14 days under shade and agitation every 2-3 days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the product with date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because of its ability to improve cardiovascular function by increasing blood flow, reducing inflammation, and relaxing the blood vessels. The oil's flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) help to strengthen the heart muscle, reduce stress on the heart, and prevent oxidative damage to the cardiac tissue. Additionally, hawthorn essential oils' anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the legs and feet, a common symptom of congestive heart failure, promoting overall cardiovascular health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic properties: Hawthorn essential oil helps to tone and strengthen the heart muscle, improving its ability to pump blood efficiently.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to congestive heart failure by reducing cardiac fibrosis and improving blood flow.
Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that damage the heart tissue, protecting it from oxidative stress and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots contain flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) that help to improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and strengthen cardiovascular health.

Barks: The barks of the hawthorn plant contain triterpenic acids that have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and stems at peak potency and store in airtight container for future use.
Combine 200g of harvested crataegus monogyna material with 1000ml of high-proof industrial ethanol in a large glass jar for maceration.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, agitating daily to ensure optimal solvent penetration and herbal extraction.
After 14 days, strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another container, discarding solids and retaining the infused ethanol.
Distill the ethanol extract using a steam distillation apparatus at 40°c for 2 hours, collecting the resulting crataegus monogyna essential oil in a glass vial.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because of its unique composition, which includes compounds that have been shown to improve cardiovascular health. The oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to heart failure. Additionally, its ability to dilate blood vessels and lower blood pressure can help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure, such as shortness of breath and swelling. Overall, maidenhair tree essential oils may provide a natural and effective way to support cardiovascular health and manage congestive heart failure.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: These properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to the development of congestive heart failure.
Cardioprotective effects: Adiantum has been shown to have a positive effect on cardiac function and structure, potentially reducing the risk of congestive heart failure.
Vasodilatory effects: The plant's bioactive compounds may help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can be beneficial for individuals with congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce blood pressure, making them effective in treating congestive heart failure.

Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help improve cardiovascular health and reduce symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees ensuring at least 200ml of leaves for processing.
Wash the harvested leaves with distilled water and gentle soap to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator set at 40°c for 24 hours or air-drying method taking 5-7 days.
Steam distill 100g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 500ml of distilled water at atmospheric pressure for 2 hours.
Filter and decant the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter into dark glass bottles using sterile techniques.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because of its remarkable ability to improve cardiovascular function. The oil's active compounds, such as ginsenosides, have been shown to enhance blood flow and reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system. This can help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure, including shortness of breath and fatigue, by improving oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. Additionally, ginseng essential oils may also help regulate blood pressure and promote healthy lipid profiles, further supporting cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Cardioprotective effects: Ginseng has been shown to help protect against cardiovascular disease by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and preventing oxidative stress, which can contribute to congestive heart failure.
Antioxidant activity: Ginseng's antioxidant properties can help reduce the damage caused by free radicals in the body, which can contribute to the progression of congestive heart failure.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Ginseng has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the inflammation that occurs in the body as a result of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of ginseng contain bioactive compounds that help regulate blood pressure and reduce inflammation, making them an ideal choice for congestive heart failure.
Rhyzomes: Ginseng rhyzomes are rich in saponins, which have been shown to improve cardiac function and reduce oxidative stress, making them effective for treating congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh panax ginseng roots of at least one year old in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency and aroma.
Wash the roots thoroughly with cold running water and dry them gently with a clean towel to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill 10 grams of dried panax ginseng root in a still apparatus using 500ml of distilled water at 100°c for 3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Filter the resulting distillate through a sterile cotton cloth and collect the essential oil that separates from the aqueous phase within 2 hours of collection.
Store the panax ginseng essential oil in dark glass vials with tight-fitting lids, sealed and stored at room temperature for up to 6 months or in the refrigerator for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because they have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow. The oil's flavonoids and terpenes also possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Additionally, broad-leaved lime essential oils may help to regulate heart rhythm and improve cardiac output, making it a valuable natural remedy for individuals with congestive heart failure seeking relief from symptoms and improved overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic activity: Herbal broad-leaved lime essential oil has cardiotonic properties, which means it can improve cardiovascular function and help reduce symptoms of congestive heart failure by strengthening the heart muscle and improving blood circulation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of herbal broad-leaved lime essential oil help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are common underlying causes of congestive heart failure and can exacerbate its symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Herbal broad-leaved lime essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to congestion and fluid buildup in the lungs and other tissues associated with congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, roots have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, making them effective in managing symptoms of congestive heart failure.
Barks: Containing phenolic compounds and tannins, barks have been traditionally used to lower blood pressure, reduce fluid buildup, and alleviate swelling associated with congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of tilia platyphyllos plant and dry them in shade for 7-10 days.
Steam distill dried leaves using a clevenger apparatus with 20 grams of material and 200ml of water.
Collect distilled essential oil and transfer it to glass vial, filtering through cheesecloth if necessary.
Add 5% geranium essential oil as a carrier blend and store in dark glass bottle for up to one year.
Use 10-15 drops of herbal essential oil blend per massage oil or bath treatment with gentle circulation movements.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The boswellic acids present in frankincense have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with congestive heart failure such as shortness of breath, swelling, and fatigue. Additionally, frankincense has been found to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure, further reducing the risk of complications from this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common underlying cause of congestive heart failure, and can alleviate symptoms such as chest pain and shortness of breath.

Antioxidant: Frankincense oil's antioxidant properties help protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of congestive heart failure, and may also help reduce scarring and fibrosis in the heart muscle.

Cardiac tonic: Frankincense oil has been traditionally used as a cardiac tonic, helping to improve blood circulation, lower blood pressure, and promote overall cardiovascular health, all of which can be beneficial for individuals with congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the frankincense plant contain bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure by reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health.

Buds: The buds of the frankincense plant are rich in oleo-gum resin, which has been shown to have cardioprotective effects and can help reduce the risk of cardiac arrhythmias associated with congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boswellia serrata leaves and stems, wash them thoroughly with distilled water, and dry them in the shade for 2-3 days.
Crush dried boswellia serrata plant material into small pieces using a mortar and pestle, then combine it with carrier oil at a ratio of 1:2.
Allow the mixture to steep in a glass container for 4-6 weeks, shaking the bottle daily, and filtering it through cheesecloth every 7 days.
Distill the resulting herbal extract using a steam distillation apparatus, collecting the essential oil as it separates from water, which yields approximately 1-2% boswellia serrata essential oil.
Store the extracted boswellia serrata essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, away from direct sunlight and heat, at a temperature of around 15-20°c for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been shown to improve blood circulation, reducing the risk of fluid buildup and swelling in the lungs and legs. Vasodilatory properties: Rosemary essential oil's ability to dilate blood vessels can help reduce blood pressure and improve circulation, alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure such as shortness of breath and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which is a common underlying condition in congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create a grounding and calming essential oil that helps alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Steam distill 200g of harvested plant material with 1l of distilled water at 100°c for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by cooling the distillation apparatus to 20°c and allowing it to settle.
Filter the separated essential oil through a coffee filter into dark glass bottles containing 5% vitamin e oil as an antioxidant.
Store the finished rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a distillation still.
Heat water in a separate vessel to generate steam, then direct it into the still.
Collect the resulting essential oil as it condenses and separates from the steam.
Filter and bottle the essential oil for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because of its remarkable ability to reduce inflammation, improve blood circulation, and lower blood pressure. remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula oil has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can help protect the cardiovascular system from damage and improve heart function.

Wound-healing and tissue repair properties: Calendula oil's ability to promote wound healing and tissue repair may help with skin integrity issues that often accompany congestive heart failure, such as edema and skin ulcers.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at peak blooming stage in early morning for optimal oil content preservation.
Clean and dry the harvested calendula flowers thoroughly using a soft-bristled brush and paper towels within 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried calendula flowers using a copper or stainless steel apparatus with a ratio of 1:4 flowers to water for 2-3 hours.
Filter the resulting oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a glass bottle, discarding any sediment and ensuring proper cap sealing within 30 minutes.
Store the calendula essential oil in a cool, dark place protected from direct sunlight and heat sources for at least 14 days before use with recommended dosage of 10-15 drops per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms such as swelling, shortness of breath, and fatigue that are common in congestive heart failure patients. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier weighing approximately 100 grams.
Clean the harvested roots thoroughly with distilled water and gently scrub off any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm oven at 60°c for exactly 6 hours to prevent moisture retention.
Steam distill the dried roots using a clevenger apparatus with 10 milliliters of water for 30 minutes at atmospheric pressure.
Filter and store the resulting essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and airtight caps for future use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat congestive heart failure with herbal essential oils is to combine Frankincense, Geranium, and Lavender oils in a massage blend. These oils help reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and promote relaxation, which alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure. Add 5-7 drops of each oil to a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and gently massage onto the chest, back, and legs three times daily. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using essential oils for medical treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to congestive heart failure that are treated with herbal essential oils are chronic cardiovascular conditions, such as hypertension, atherosclerosis, and peripheral artery disease. Certain herbs like rosemary, thyme, and hawthorn berries have been used traditionally to help improve circulation, reduce blood pressure, and prevent platelet aggregation. These properties may help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure by promoting healthy heart function.

By increasing the strength of heart contractions and improving the heart's ability to pump blood, Digitalis purpurea essential oils can help alleviate fatigue, shortness of breath, and swelling associated with congestive heart failure. remedy_constituents: Cardiac glycosides: Help with congestive heart failure by increasing the force of cardiac contractions and improving the heart's pumping ability.

remedies.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because it has been traditionally used to support cardiovascular health by increasing cardiac output and improving blood circulation. The oil's flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) have been shown to have vasodilatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce blood pressure and prevent oxidative stress. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help to improve heart function and reduce symptoms of congestive heart failure by inhibiting the breakdown of norepinephrine, a neurotransmitter that plays a crucial role in regulating heart rate and blood pressure.

Phenolic acids: These antioxidants help to protect the heart from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to the progression of congestive heart failure by reducing damage to cardiac tissue and improving overall cardiovascular health.

Triterpenoids: These compounds have been shown to improve heart function and reduce symptoms of congestive heart failure by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that break down norepinephrine, leading to improved heart rate and blood pressure regulation.

Fruits: The fruits, particularly the berries, contain flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) that have been shown to have a positive effect on heart failure by improving cardiovascular function and reducing oxidative stress.

Buds: The buds of Crataegus monogyna are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to improve cardiovascular function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall heart health, making them useful in managing congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the crataegus monogyna leaves flowers and fruits at dawn when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from the dried plant material.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and improve clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because it promotes relaxation and reduces stress, which are common contributors to heart disease. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties may also help alleviate conditions such as high blood pressure and arrhythmias, both of which can exacerbate heart failure. remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to congestive heart failure.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits vasodilatory and anti-arrhythmic properties, which can help improve heart function and reduce symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Caryophyllene oxide: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system and alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most used part of Lavandula angustifolia for essential oils due to their high content of linalool and linalyl acetate, which contribute to the plant's calming and relaxing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are also used for essential oils, as they contain similar compounds to the flowers, but in smaller amounts.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia can be used for essential oils, but they are less commonly used than flowers or leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers on a sunny day when they are in full bloom.
Steam distill the harvested flowers in a copper still for 2-3 hours to release their oil.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from water using a separating funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the collected essential oil through a filter paper or cotton to remove impurities.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because it improves blood circulation and reduces inflammation. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late summer to early fall.
Dry the leaves in a warm oven at 150°f for 2-3 hours to remove moisture.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a distillation apparatus to extract essential oils.

Store the ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers at peak potency in early morning for highest oil quality.
Steam distill 250g of rosmarinus officinalis plant material in 1 liter of distilled water for 2 hours.
Collect the condensed steam distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and contaminants from the oil.
Store the extracted rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because of its adaptogenic and vasodilatory properties. The oil's active compound, valepotriates, helps to lower blood pressure and reduce stress on the heart, alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure. Additionally, its sedative and anti-inflammatory effects promote relaxation, reducing strain on the heart and improving overall cardiovascular health. remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This constituent has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the heart and alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid has been found to possess vasodilatory effects, which can help improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, thereby alleviating some symptoms of congestive heart failure.
Valeranone: Valeranone has been demonstrated to exhibit sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, common comorbidities associated with congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of valerenic acid and other active compounds that contribute to their medicinal properties.
Barks: Some producers use the bark of Valeriana officinalis to create essential oils, which may contain unique combinations of compounds with potential health benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots and stems in the early morning to avoid moisture accumulation.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried plant material at 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 3-4 hours to release essential oils.
Collect the essential oils and filter them through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the valeriana officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 6 months.

Additionally, Ruscus aculeatus essential oil may help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can contribute to a reduction in blood pressure and an overall improvement in cardiovascular health. This may lead to better management of congestive heart failure.
remedy_constituents: Furostanol saponins: These compounds help with congestive heart failure by reducing fluid buildup in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as edema and shortness of breath.

Steroidal saponins: These compounds have a diuretic effect, which helps to remove excess fluid from the body and alleviate pressure on the heart, thereby reducing the risk of congestive heart failure.

Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the heart from damage and reduce inflammation, ultimately contributing to the prevention and management of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used in essential oil production due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
Roots: Used in essential oil production for their diuretic and cardiotonic properties, which can help reduce fluid buildup and support heart function in congestive heart failure.
Stems: Used in essential oil production for their vasodilatory properties, which can help reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ruscus aculeatus leaves and stems in the early morning to minimize moisture content.
Dry the harvested ruscus aculeatus leaves and stems in a warm place for 2-3 weeks.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from the dried ruscus aculeatus material.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the water during the steam distillation process.
Filter the essential oil to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because of its unique properties that promote cardiovascular health. The oil's triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system. By improving blood flow, reducing blood pressure, and enhancing the heart's pumping efficiency, Centella asiatica essential oils support the heart's function and overall well-being, making it a valuable natural remedy for congestive heart failure patients.
remedy_constituents: Madecassoside: Helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the heart, improving cardiovascular function and reducing symptoms of congestive heart failure.
Madecassol: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the heart from damage and improve its pumping efficiency in congestive heart failure patients.

Asiatic acid: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and antifibrotic effects, which help reduce scarring and inflammation in the heart, improving its function and reducing symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Centella asiatica leaves are used due to their high content of triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular-protective properties.

Roots: Centella asiatica roots are used because they contain a high concentration of compounds such as asiatic acid and madecassic acid, which have been found to have potential in treating cardiovascular diseases.

Stems: Centella asiatica stems are used due to their content of triterpenoids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may help in managing congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest centella asiatica leaves and stems in the morning when the plant is at its highest concentration of active compounds.
Dry the harvested plant material in a temperature-controlled environment at 50-60 degrees celsius for 24-48 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a steam distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a separation funnel and transfer it to a glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or a centrifuge to remove any remaining impurities and particles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because it supports cardiovascular health by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and enhancing the body's natural response to oxidative stress. The oil's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, which can contribute to heart failure. Additionally, it has been traditionally used to boost the immune system, reduce fatigue, and promote overall well-being, making it a valuable complementary therapy for managing congestive heart failure symptoms. Regular use may help alleviate symptoms and improve quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Astragaloside IV: Astragaloside IV has been shown to improve heart function and reduce inflammation in the heart, which can help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Astragalus membranaceus have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the heart from damage and reduce the risk of congestive heart failure.

Saponins: Saponins in Astragalus membranaceus have been found to have a positive effect on heart function and may help to improve cardiac output and reduce blood pressure, which can help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to its adaptogenic properties and potential to improve heart function.
Rhyzomes: Used due to its potential to improve heart function and reduce inflammation.
Barks: Used due to its potential to lower blood pressure and improve heart health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried astragalus membranaceus roots and separate them into small pieces to increase surface area.
Steam distill the prepared roots at 100°c for 2 hours to release essential oil components slowly.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the distilled water using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any remaining impurities completely.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 5°c to preserve its potency for months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng essential oils helps with congestive heart failure because it improves cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. The oil's antioxidant properties protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals, while its anti-inflammatory compounds help alleviate symptoms such as edema and fatigue. Additionally, Panax ginseng has been shown to increase nitric oxide production, which helps to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, ultimately contributing to a reduction in blood pressure and improved heart function.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpene saponins help alleviate congestive heart failure by improving cardiovascular function, reducing inflammation, and inhibiting oxidative stress.

Sesquiterpenes: These compounds, including ginsenoside F2 and panaxadiol, have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory effects, which help reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms associated with congestive heart failure.

Phenolic acids: These compounds, including ferulic acid and caffeic acid, have antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, thereby reducing the risk and progression of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are primarily used due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to have cardiovascular benefits.
Rhyzomes: They are also used because they contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including ginsenosides, that may help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
Leaves: They are used in some formulations due to their antioxidant properties, which can help protect the heart from oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh panax ginseng roots that are 4-6 years old for best quality and potency.
Dry the ginseng roots in a warm and well-ventilated area at 45-55°c for 2-3 days.
Steam distill the dried ginseng roots at 80-100°c to separate the essential oils from the plant material.
Collect the essential oils that are separated from the plant material and store them in a cool dark place.
Filter the essential oils through a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove any impurities and debris.
